facts: Summer is the hottest season and winter is the coldest because the sun's warmth changes with the seasons, so we wear shorts in summer and coats in winter.
Spring is warmer than winter but cooler than summer; plants begin to grow as the temperature rises.
Autumn is cooler than summer but warmer than winter; leaves change color and fall from trees as the weather cools.
Weather can change from day to day; a cloudy day might bring rain, but it might also stay dry if the clouds are not rain clouds.
misconceptions: If the sky is cloudy, it always means rain will come soon.
Spring and autumn have the same weather—neither is warmer or cooler than the other.
